title: Executive Order 10464—Delegation of Functions of the President Respecting the Transfer of Wheat to Pakistan

flags.justification: Sec. 2 explicitly excludes the termination-of-assistance function from the delegation, reserving it to the President — a deliberate limiting feature.
A functional delegation implementing a specific one-time congressional act.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
Implements a specific, bounded, one-time congressional act.
Sec. 4 explicitly subordinates the delegated functions to the Secretary of State's foreign-policy responsibilities — a real, cross-cutting oversight-preservation feature.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A narrow, specific, bounded implementation of a single wheat-transfer program to one country.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. Notable for two positive design features that recur in later Eisenhower-era delegation orders: reserving the most consequential function (termination) to the President, and subordinating delegated authority to the Secretary of State's foreign-policy role.
